page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB is a KVM-hypervised virtual server configured with 32 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM. The system is powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared, utilizing 32 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 12.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this server type.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured by stress-ng is poor, placing in the bottom 10%, and Geekbench text processing is weak, falling in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance is more resilient, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores both registering in the average range. Notably, the server demonstrates strong performance in the top 25% for multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU encryption tests. Memory bandwidth and database operations consistently perform in the average tier.

The primary tradeoff of this server is its shared CPU allocation and low single-core clock speed, which limits its suitability for single-threaded tasks. However, the high memory density of 384.0 GB offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. This profile is suited for large-scale memory caching, multi-threaded database operations, and memory-heavy general-purpose workloads that can tolerate shared CPU resources.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 32 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, running on a KVM hypervisor. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz, the instance features a shared CPU allocation with 32 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. It does not include local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth fall within the average range. The server excels in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and CPU encryption, placing in the top 25%. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 12.0 GB per core, this instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and multi-threaded workloads that can leverage large memory capacities under a shared CPU resource model.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB is a KVM-based server with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384.0 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-threaded decompression but exhibits poor single-core performance.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB features 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384.0 GB RAM, offering average multi-core and poor single-core performance.
tagline: A high-memory virtual server featuring thirty-two shared vCPUs and three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing 12.0 GB per core
Strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and CPU encryption performance
Poor single-core CPU performance and weak text processing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for memory-intensive databases and multi-threaded workloads
